title: Postpartum Hemorrhage - Uterine Atony
chief_complaint: Just delivered vaginally 30 minutes ago. Now having heavy vaginal bleeding - soaking through pads every few minutes. Blood is bright red and continuous.

physical_exam.Abdomen: Uterus soft, boggy, above umbilicus (atony - fundus not firm). Expressing large clots.
physical_exam.Pelvic: Heavy vaginal bleeding (bright red). Cervix and vaginal lacerations ruled out (examined). Placenta delivered intact (3 vessels, complete).
physical_exam.General: Pale, diaphoretic. Class II hemorrhagic shock.

hidden_data.uterotonics: Oxytocin 40U IV bolus given, Methergine 0.2mg IM given, Hemabate 250mcg IM given. Uterus still boggy.

correct_diagnosis: Primary Postpartum Hemorrhage from Uterine Atony - Refractory to First-Line Uterotonics
key_learning_points: Postpartum hemorrhage = blood loss >500mL (vaginal) or >1,000mL (C-section) within 24h of delivery. Leading cause of maternal death worldwide (25%). 4 Ts: Tone (atony - 70%), Trauma (lacerations, rupture, inversion - 20%), Tissue (retained placenta - 10%), Thrombin (coagulopathy - 1%)

Uterine atony = failure of uterus to contract after delivery. Risk factors: Prolonged labor, Rapid labor, Overdistended uterus (multiple gestation, polyhydramnios, macrosomia), Grand multiparity, Chorioamnionitis, Magnesium sulfate, Halogenated anesthetics, Tocolytic agents

Management (sequential - move to next if fails): 1) Uterine massage (fundal massage - firm, circular). 2) Uterotonics: Oxytocin 20-40U in 1L NS at 250mL/hr (first-line). Methergine 0.2mg IM (contraindicated: HTN, preeclampsia - causes vasospasm). Hemabate (Carboprost) 250mcg IM q15min up to 8 doses (contraindicated: Asthma - bronchospasm). Misoprostol 800-1,000mcg PR (off-label, less effective). 3) Tranexamic acid (TXA) 1g IV within 3h (WOMAN trial - reduces mortality from PPH by 30%). 4) Uterine tamponade: Bakri balloon (inflate 300-500mL NS), Foley catheter (multiple), or uterine packing. 5) Surgical: B-Lynch compression suture, Uterine artery ligation (O'Leary stitch), Bilateral hypogastric artery ligation. 6) Hysterectomy (last resort - life-saving)

MTP activation: >1,500mL blood loss + ongoing bleeding + hemodynamic instability. 1:1:1 ratio pRBC:FFP:Platelets. Cryoprecipitate if fibrinogen <200 (pregnancy normally high fibrinogen 400-600, so <200 is critical). Calcium replacement (citrate toxicity from massive transfusion). Prevent hypothermia (fluid warmers, Bair Hugger). Tranexamic acid 1g IV q8h

Quantify blood loss: Collect all blood in drapes/bags, weigh pads (1g = 1mL blood). Communicate with team: 'I need help. This is a PPH with estimated blood loss of X. We need OB, Anesthesia, OR team, and blood bank STAT.'
